analysis.problem: TypeScript compilation error in TestimonialsCarousel.tsx: The PostSelectorSortable component's onChange callback has the signature `(ids: number[] | number) => void`, but the current handler at lines 162-164 passes the value directly to setAttributes({ selectedPosts: value }). The selectedPosts attribute is typed as `number[]` (line 36), so assigning `number | number[]` to it causes a type error: 'Type number | number[] is not assignable to type number[]'.
analysis.rootCause: The PostSelectorSortable component (from @taktdev/components) is designed to handle both single-select (returns a number) and multi-select (returns a number[]) modes. Its onChange type is `(ids: number[] | number) => void`. When used without a `limit` prop (multi-select mode), it typically returns a number[], but the type system requires handling both cases.

referencePattern.note: DynamicContentCarousel had the identical issue and was fixed with this exact pattern. The fix was verified to work correctly.

changes.description: Add type guard to PostSelectorSortable onChange handler to safely coerce the `number | number[]` return value into `number[]` before assigning to selectedPosts attribute.
changes.currentCode:
<PostSelectorSortable
	value={ attributes.selectedPosts }
	onChange={ ( value ) =>
		setAttributes( { selectedPosts: value } )
	}
	postTypes={ [ 'testimonial' ] }
/>
changes.proposedCode:
<PostSelectorSortable
	value={ attributes.selectedPosts }
	onChange={ ( value ) =>
		setAttributes( {
			selectedPosts: Array.isArray( value )
				? value
				: value
					? [ value ]
					: [],
		} )
	}
	postTypes={ [ 'testimonial' ] }
/>
changes.rationale: Array.isArray(value) handles the number[] case (pass through). The ternary `value ? [value] : []` handles the number case: wrap a valid post ID in an array, or return empty array for 0/falsy values. This is the identical pattern successfully applied to DynamicContentCarousel.

decision.decision: Use Array.isArray() type guard with falsy check for single number values
decision.evidence: DynamicContentCarousel planning-2026-02-12-153116.json used the same fix pattern for the identical issue. FeaturedPost.tsx uses the inverse pattern (typeof check) for single-select mode.

otherOnChangeHandlersAudit.description: Audit of all other onChange handlers in TestimonialsCarousel.tsx for similar type issues

handlers.code: onChange={ ( value ) => setAttributes( { postsSource: value as string } ) }
handlers.note: Uses `as string` cast. ToggleGroupControl onChange returns `string | number | undefined`. The cast is acceptable since all options are strings.
TaxonomyMultiSelect onChange type is separate from PostSelectorSortable. Not in scope for this fix.
RangeControl onChange returns `number | undefined`. Minor potential undefined issue but not in scope.
ToggleControl onChange returns boolean, matches attribute type.
Custom onChange with Partial<TestimonialsCarouselAttributes> type. Properly typed.

otherOnChangeHandlersAudit.summary: Only one onChange handler (PostSelectorSortable at lines 160-167) has the type mismatch issue. All other handlers are correctly typed or use acceptable casts.

testPlan.description: This is a type-safety-only fix. No visual or functional changes occur.
steps: Verify TypeScript compilation succeeds after the change (no type errors in TestimonialsCarousel.tsx)
Verify the block editor still allows selecting and deselecting testimonials in manual mode
Verify selectedPosts attribute is correctly saved as an array after selection
testPlan.reason: Pure TypeScript type guard addition with no runtime behavior change. The PostSelectorSortable already returns arrays in multi-select mode; this fix just satisfies the type checker.
